tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria, suggesting possible glomerular damage. ALT is mildly elevated, which may reflect hepatic involvement. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with glucosuria, ketonuria, and low specific gravity, raising concern for possible diabetic nephropathy or acute kidney injury. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
